refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with end-to-end communication latency that ensures safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is mostly atomic, focusing on teleoperation latency and its compatibility with safety. The phrase 'compatible with safe remote operation' is kept to emphasize the safety aspect without specifying how latency is achieved or measured, maintaining solution neutrality. The FR does not contain multiple obligations requiring splitting.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the end-to-end communication latency during teleoperation under agreed network conditions and verify that the latency meets the safety criteria defined in the worksite safety plan and operational policies, specifically that the p95 latency is ≤ 150 ms.

candidate_dps: Implement a communication protocol optimized for low-latency end-to-end data transfer
Design a system architecture that prioritizes real-time data processing and minimal buffering
Incorporate latency monitoring and adaptive control mechanisms to maintain latency within threshold
Separate teleoperation control commands from non-critical data streams to reduce communication load
Use predictive control algorithms to compensate for communication delays
selected_dp.selected_dp: Design a system architecture that prioritizes real-time data processing and minimal buffering

potential_sources: Tight integration between communication protocol and control system
Shared resources causing delays in data processing
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on architectural design to minimize latency, which supports independence from specific communication protocols or predictive algorithms, reducing coupling risk.

assumptions_risks: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters to meet latency requirements
Operational policies do not introduce additional delays or constraints
Latency measurement and monitoring are accurate and timely
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including adaptive latency monitoring and control mechanisms as a complementary DP to enhance robustness against variable network conditions.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the core system architecture responsible for real-time data processing and minimal buffering, integrating necessary components and managing data flows.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Bi-directional data exchange for real-time telemetry data essential for processing.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Network communication interface supporting real-time data transmission and reception.

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as NGHE Solution is the core system handling data processing; minimal diffusion as most processing logic is centralized here.
Vehicle module may handle some data but primarily focused on physical vehicle functions; significant diffusion if DP placed here.
Teleoperation involves remote control and may require some real-time data, but not the primary processing hub.
Safety Services focus on alerts and safety monitoring, not on general real-time data processing; high diffusion risk.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary to contain the DP as it centralizes real-time data processing and buffering management, minimizing diffusion and maximizing cohesion.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on external modules for data input/output may introduce latency or buffering outside the boundary.
If real-time constraints are not strictly enforced, buffering might inadvertently spread to other modules.
Integration complexity with Telemetry and Network Link interfaces may cause unforeseen coupling.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: If containment proves weak, consider refining the DP to explicitly separate data acquisition and processing stages, allowing clearer module boundaries and reducing diffusion.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP logically addresses the FR by focusing on system architecture that prioritizes real-time data processing and minimal buffering, which directly supports achieving low end-to-end communication latency necessary for safe teleoperation.

diffusion_risks: Potential dependency on external modules for data input/output may introduce latency or buffering outside the NGHE Solution boundary.
If real-time constraints are not strictly enforced, buffering might inadvertently spread to other modules such as Vehicle or Teleoperation.
Integration complexity with Telemetry and Network Link interfaces may cause unforeseen coupling and diffusion risks.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high cohesion and centralizes the DP, minimizing diffusion. Other modules have lower containment scores and higher diffusion risks if the DP were placed there. Overall, the IM boundary is appropriate but requires careful management of external interfaces to avoid diffusion.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible by measuring end-to-end latency (p95 ≤ 150 ms) under agreed network conditions and validating compliance with worksite safety plans and operational policies.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the NGHE Solution IM boundary is clear, with well-defined interfaces (Telemetry and Network Link) supporting the functional requirement for teleoperation latency.

recommended_revisions: Consider refining the DP to explicitly separate data acquisition and processing stages to clarify module boundaries and reduce diffusion risk.
Monitor and manage dependencies on external modules to ensure buffering and latency constraints remain within the NGHE Solution boundary.
Enhance interface definitions and integration strategies for Telemetry and Network Link to minimize coupling and potential diffusion.

decision.justification: Although the current FR, DP, and IM mappings pass all gates with clear logical and architectural alignment, the high safety criticality and identified diffusion risks necessitate further decomposition. Refining the DP to separate data acquisition and processing stages will clarify module boundaries and reduce diffusion risk. This next-level decomposition will enable more precise allocation and verification of sub-functions, improving safety assurance and manageability of dependencies and interfaces.
next_level_sub_frs: Acquire real-time data with minimal buffering
Process data to ensure low latency communication
Manage interfaces with Telemetry and Network Link modules

revise.why: To explicitly separate data acquisition and processing stages, clarifying module boundaries and reducing diffusion risk as recommended.

assumptions: The NGHE Solution module can be decomposed into sub-functions without violating architectural constraints.
Interfaces with external modules can be better managed through refined sub-FRs and DPs.
open_risks: Potential latency or buffering introduced by dependencies on external modules.
Integration complexity and coupling with Telemetry and Network Link interfaces.
Ensuring strict enforcement of real-time constraints across sub-modules.
evidence_needed: Performance measurements of sub-module latency and buffering.
Verification results of interface definitions and integration strategies.
Safety compliance validation under refined sub-FR allocations.
